content.problemStatement:
You are given an N√óNN \times NN√óN grid. Each cell in the grid contains a tree with a positive integer height.

You also have a machine that operates with a positive integer parameter KKK. When you apply the machine to a row, every tree in that row with height greater than KKK will have its height reduced to exactly KKK; trees with height less than or equal to KKK remain unchanged.

For any row iii (where 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N), define its transformed sum as:

F(i)=sum of all tree heights in row i after applying the machine.F(i) = \text{sum of all tree heights in row } i \text{ after applying the machine}.F(i)=sum of all tree heights in row i after applying the machine.The score of a first row is defined as the number of rows in the grid that have a transformed sum strictly greater than F(1)F(1)F(1), i.e. number of iii (2‚â§i‚â§N2 \le i \le N2‚â§i‚â§N) such that F(i)>F(1)F(i) > F(1)F(i)>F(1).

Your task is to determine the minimum positive integer value of KKK such that the score of the first row is maximized.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
Each of the next NNN lines contains NNN space-separated integers: Hi,1,Hi,2,‚Ä¶,Hi,NH_{i,1}, H_{i,2}, \dots, H_{i,N}Hi,1‚Äã,Hi,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Hi,N‚Äã, where the ithi_{th}ith‚Äã line corresponds to the ithi_{th}ith‚Äã row of the grid.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum positive integer value of KKK such that the score of the first row is maximized.
constraints: The sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: At K=6K = 6K=6, any tree taller than 666 is reduced to 666. In the grid, only the value H3,1H_{3,1}H3,1‚Äã (in the third row) is affected, becoming 666.
The sums after this operation are:

First row = 101010.
Second row = 999.
Third row = 6+4+1=116 + 4 + 1 = 116+4+1=11.

Now, only the third row has a sum greater than the first row, so the first row's score is 111. It can be shown that this is the maximum possible score for the first row, and K=6K = 6K=6 is the smallest value that achieves it.
